conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children. I'm asking about both of them - Alex, who's 12, and Blake, who's 8.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have two children - Alex (age 12) and Blake (age 8).

Now I need to ask you a few questions to determine eligibility for both children.

First, are you responsible for Alex and Blake? By this I mean, do they normally live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you.

Next question: Are either Alex or Blake currently in any of the following situations:
- Living in hospital or residential care
- Living with someone else (even temporarily)
- In local authority care

Or do they both live with you full-time at home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They both live with me full-time at home. Neither of them is in hospital, residential care, or local authority care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Now, an important question: Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you.

One more question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake? (For example, another parent or guardian?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's the summary:

**For Alex (age 12):** ✅ **Eligible**
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Alex and they live with you full-time in the UK
- Alex is not in hospital or care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**For Blake (age 8):** ✅ **Eligible**
- Blake is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Blake and they live with you full-time in the UK
- Blake is not in hospital or care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can claim for both children, and you'll receive payments for each of them (the rate is higher for the eldest child and a lower rate for additional children).

If you'd like to proceed, you can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 12 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant full-time in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Alex, Alex is not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can get Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) and you live in the UK. Alex meets all these criteria, so the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant full-time in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Blake, Blake is not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can get Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) and you live in the UK. Blake meets all these criteria, so the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for two children - Alex (12) and Blake (8) - who both live with them full-time. Neither child is in hospital, residential care, or local authority care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. Both children are under 16 years old. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.
